sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: FC Augsburg, sitting at 11th in the league with 43 points, host Holstein Kiel, who are struggling in 17th position with only 25 points in Round 32 at the WWK ARENA in Augsburg. This matchup pits a mid-table side against a team fighting to avoid relegation. The referee, Harm Osmers, is known for averaging 4.2 yellow cards and 0.21 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially heated encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last meeting on December 21, 2024, Holstein Kiel dominated FC Augsburg with a stunning 5-1 victory at home. Shuto Machino was the standout performer in that game, earning a Sofascore rating of 9.1. Over the last 5 matchups between these teams, results have been split with 1 win each for both sides and no draws, indicating competitive fixtures.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Statistics
sections.text: FC Augsburg have scored 34 goals this season, ranking 14th in the league, while conceding 45 goals, placing them 6th for least goals conceded. Their ability to create big chances is poor, with only 50 created (18th in the league), though they miss the fewest big chances (33, ranked 1st). Holstein Kiel, on the other hand, are in poor form defensively, with a streak of 8 games without a clean sheet and 7 out of 8 games where both teams scored. Their matches also tend to see over 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 outings.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - FC Augsburg
sections.text: For FC Augsburg, forward Alexis Claude-Maurice has been impressive, scoring 9 goals against an expected goals (xG) of 4.25, overperforming by 4.75. Defender Keven Schlotterbeck stands out with a league rank of 3 for ground duels won at 71.7%, while Chrislain Matsima ranks 5th for aerial duels won at 70.3%. Midfielder Frank Onyeka is notable for being fouled 2.5 times per game, ranking 2nd in the league. Players like Phillip Tietz, with an average Sofascore rating of 6.94 over the last 5 matchups against Holstein Kiel, and Samuel Essende, with 7 goals, will also be crucial.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Holstein Kiel
sections.text: Holstein Kiel’s attack is led by Shuto Machino, who has scored 11 goals against an xG of 5.87, overperforming by 5.13, and is in form with 3 goals in his last 5 matches at a Sofascore rating of 7.52. Alexander Bernhardsson, another forward, has also contributed 7 goals this season and 3 in his last 5 matches. Phil Harres adds depth to their attack with 8 goals, while Steven Skrzybski has chipped in with 6 goals and 3 assists. Jann-Fiete Arp, despite only 2 goals, remains a potential threat despite ranking 5th for long balls in total passes at 9.6%.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Absences
sections.text: FC Augsburg boast strong defensive stats with 11 clean sheets (3rd in the league) and lead the league in interceptions per game at 10.25. Their aerial duels won percentage of 52.38% (5th in the league) could be key against Holstein Kiel. However, Holstein Kiel are missing key players like Finn Porath and Lewis Holtby, which could hamper their midfield creativity. Augsburg’s ability to score early, having been first to score in 5 of their last 7 games, might give them an edge over Kiel, who have scored first in 4 of their last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Scoring Patterns and Game Dynamics
sections.text: Analyzing goal distribution, FC Augsburg tend to score most between the 16th-30th and 46th-60th minutes, with 7 goals in each period, accounting for 41% of their total goals. Holstein Kiel peak late, with 16 goals between the 76th-90th minutes, representing 16% of their tally, alongside 10 goals between the 31st-45th minutes. This suggests Augsburg might dominate early, while Kiel could push for late goals. With Augsburg’s matches often seeing less than 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8, and Kiel’s high-scoring trend, this game could be a tactical battle.
